last_item: alert — handleTestAlertChannel (internal/server/handlers_alerts.go:291-342) had NO handler-level test. Iter #12 flagged a suspected nil-deref (the credential switch at :327 has no default, so a ch.Kind outside {pushover,webhook} would leave notifier nil and panic on SendTest) but concluded it's UNREACHABLE, gated by two guards: (1) req.Kind validated to pushover|webhook at :300, (2) ch selected only when channels[i].Kind == req.Kind at :313. Band-2 / Mode-A: convert the analysis into a test that pins BOTH guards so a future refactor can't reintroduce the panic.
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): +3 tests in NEW internal/server/handlers_alerts_test.go, all driving the REAL router POST /api/alerts/channels/test via authedRequest + newTestServer, seeding channels through the real store config key (writeAlertChannels -> SetConfig(alertChannelsConfigKey) -> the loadAlertChannelsCtx read path). (1) _RejectsUnknownKind: kind='slack' -> 400/CodeValidation, message contains both 'pushover' and 'webhook', before any channel load (pins guard 1). (2) _RequiresChannelOfExactRequestedKind: only a webhook configured (URL http://127.0.0.1:1 unreachable), request pushover -> 404/CodeNotFound, no dispatch (pins guard 2; the ONLY NotFound path is ch==nil, so any mis-selection picks a real channel and reds as 500 ExecError or 400, never 404). (3) _DispatchesToMatchingChannel: matching webhook -> real httptest server, asserts hit exactly once (atomic.Int32) with event='test' (atomic.Value) -> non-tautological, catches never-dispatch AND wrong-payload. No bug -> tests lock the contract. Mutation-proven over 4 mutations aimed at the TEST-handler copies (lines 291-342): drop validation (->404 RED), ==->!= (->500 ExecError RED), select-first if true (->500 RED), message-text change (->substring RED). TRAP: validation (250/300) and selection (263/313) lines are DUPLICATED in the save handler; first non-/g perl mutated the WRONG (save) copy -> false green (Iter #13 learning bit here); re-aimed via perl $. range 291..342, all RED. A stray un-reverted 'if true' mutation briefly failed the full suite -> caught via go test exit 1 + git diff, reverted, re-verified. code-reviewer clean (real router/store path, race-free atomics, correct cleanup ordering); test-skeptic found NO escaping mutation (verified test 2's 404-signal robust 3 ways incl. compound switch req.Kind). Backend gate green: gofmt clean, go vet OK, go test ./... exit 0 (0 FAIL), static CGO_ENABLED=0 build OK. Web untouched. e2e N/A (unit + Docker unavailable).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
